query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve:

**Current Level (EIA WCSSTUS1):**
- **319.489 million barrels** as of the latest weekly data (week ending **July 3, 2026**) — the lowest since April 1983 (43-year low)
- The last **June** reading was **325.655 million barrels** (week ending June 26, 2026)

**Weekly Trajectory (past 3–6 months):** Overwhelmingly **releases, not fills**.
- Flat-to-slightly-rising through Q1 2026, peaking at ~415.4 million barrels in mid-March
- Then an accelerating drawdown of ~95.9 million barrels over ~15 weeks
- Weekly draws ramped from <2 million bbl/week (early April) to a sustained **7–10 million bbl/week** (mid-April–late June), easing to ~6 million bbl/week in early July

**Cause:** A deliberate policy release — in March 2026 the administration announced tapping **172 million barrels** (part of a coordinated IEA ~400 million barrel release) to offset supply disruption from the Iran war and lower fuel prices. Energy Secretary Wright signaled intent to refill ~40 million barrels post-conflict, but the reserve was still drawing down as of July 3.

One data note worth flagging to the caller: WCSSTUS1 is an EIA/DOE portal series, not a native FRED series (FRED returned no match), but the values are fully corroborated by Reuters, DOE, and YCharts.

sources.snippet: SPR at 340.3 million barrels as of June 12; 8.9 million barrels released the prior week.

sources.title: Energy Sec Wright: We will refill the SPR by 40M barrels after Iran conflict is over
sources.snippet: Week ending May 29 saw 8 million barrel release to 357.1 million; Wright pledges 40M barrel refill post-conflict.

sources.snippet: Total US inventories (commercial + SPR) fell to 734 million barrels as of June 26, lowest since 1984.
